reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending a data column sidecar with excessive KZG commitments (e.g., 100 commitments exceeding MaxBlobsPerBlock of 6-12). The gossip validation (validateDataColumn → ValidFields → VerifyDataColumnSidecar at p2p_interface.go:34-52) checks length consistency but does NOT enforce len(kzg_commitments) <= MaxBlobsPerBlock. The MaxBlobsPerBlock check only exists in DataColumnsAlignWithBlock (verification.go:28-38), which is called after block receipt, not during gossip validation. This causes nodes to perform expensive KZG proof verification (validateWithKzgBatchVerifier at validate_data_column.go:148) on oversized columns, leading to CPU/memory exhaustion. Severity: High based on single-packet DoS affecting >33% of network (Prysm's ~31% share allows network-wide impact).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/fulu/p2p-interface.md#verify_data_column_sidecar - spec requires validation but implementation omits max_blobs_per_block check
